Unlimited exp candies is a ridiculously stupid thing to add, it just makes the game way too easy if you dont want to work. Don't even get me started on how bad the textures look and how poorly the game runs. New pokemon designs are uninspired. It used to take TPC a month to design each pokemon, now they just throw slop at a wall and don't even wait to see what sticks. What a disappointment.

Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

This game is an embarrassment for a franchise that once defined handheld RPGs. On the Nintendo Switch — a console that has delivered technical marvels like Breath of the Wild and Super Mario Odyssey — this game looks and runs like a rushed beta.Performance: Constant frame drops, glitches, and visual bugs plague the experience. The open world is riddled with pop-in, broken textures, and laughably poor animations. It feels unfinished, as if Game Freak had no idea how to develop for modern hardware.Graphics: Bland environments, flat lighting, and character models that look outdated even by 3DS standards. The Switch has proven it can handle vast, beautiful worlds, but Scarlet settles for mediocrity.Gameplay: While the open-world concept had potential, it’s wasted on repetitive objectives, shallow exploration, and a lack of meaningful progression. The “freedom” is an illusion — the game funnels you into predictable battles and fetch quests.Story & Characters: Forgettable, uninspired, and padded with dialogue that adds nothing. Rivals and NPCs are caricatures, offering no emotional weight or memorable arcs.Overall Polish: Scarlet feels like a cash grab, banking on the Pokémon brand rather than delivering a product worthy of the Switch era.Verdict: Pokémon Scarlet is the worst kind of disappointment — a flagship title that fails to meet even basic standards of quality. On a console capable of breathtaking experiences, Scarlet stands out as a shameful reminder of how far the series has fallen.

You can explore, but you are not significantly rewarded, which makes the experience **** the end, it seems that the franchise learned nothing from The Legend of Zelda, which showed how to give freedom and reward each choice. Scarlet starts well, but quickly gets lost in technical flaws and design **** one point, I just wanted this suffering to end.

Playing this game feels like playing a PS2 game like HL2 on a high-end PC. The game runs great now, but the textures aren't the best. All the NPC designs except for Larry and Sada are mid, and the textures for some of the Pokémon don't look good. This game has a clear set path but is disguised as an open-world game. I hope they never do an open-world Pokémon game again unless it's like the Legends games, where it's good.

Pokemon games are very hard to rate because they've never really added anything super major or insanely exciting with each iteration. And they haven't really been the best games from an objective standpoint for a long time now. But, they are still, somehow, probably the best monster tamer games on the market. It's really surprising and kind of sad that all the other ones are so mediocre they can't even come close to a series that keeps rehashing the same game over and over again. But that's the sad world us monster tamer fans must live in.

If there was any monster tamers out there that remotely came close to Pokemon, Scarlet/Violet would be an easy 5. But since there's not, you kinda gotta give it a 7. Just because if you want a monster tamer, it's really the only game in town. Even now in 2025 and likely for the foreseeable future unless some studio shocks us and makes a Pokemon killer one day. 

But as for Scarlet/Violet, sandwiches are cool. I wish Pokemon were harder to catch but with the number of Pokemon now hitting way over 1,000, it's fully understandable why they're becoming easier and easier to catch. Because I put 150 hours into Violet and I only have 1/3 of all Pokemon. Also, Wonder Trade and Hacked Pokemon is what killed anything besides collecting Pokemon in the mainline Pokemon games. You can't trade with literally anyone online except real life friends who you know for a fact didn't hack pokemon or trade with strangers online. And battling is a complete joke as well as online trading because they're both filled with nothing but computer generated fake pokemon. So the only thing worth doing in mainline Pokemon games is slowly trying to catch em' all by yourself like I do or with trusted friends who you know don't trade online, hack or cheat. 

SCORE: 7 (Good) Only because there's literally no other monster tamers on the market worth playing.
Is good. I like it. Was no at all what I expected from the bad reviews. Its really unfair.

If the graphics were actually graphics and the game actually performed properly this would be a top 3 pokemon game, but it gets to sit nice and comfortably in their bottom 3 games along with BDSP and XY

Pokemon Scarlet embodies every mistake that an open world game can make. The world is technically open from the start, but freedom to explore does little when the world is empty and there is not much to do, no interesting side quest, no proper interactive NPCs, no interesting landmarks to explore. During the game you hear about cool places that the region have, but when arriving to those places you surely end up being disappointed due the ugly and uninspiring reality. Similarly towns you arrive during the game all feel unoriginal without any specific identity to them. All buildings are copy pasted and NPCs don´t have any interesting dialogue making towns feel really lifeless. In all honestly the game lacks any incentive to go beyond the set path. For the biggest region of the series Paldea feels really small and uninteresting.

Not to mention the lack of difficulty scaling for challenges. First time in the series you have a possibility to combat gyms in any order, which sounds good on paper, but outside specific challenges, player is incentivized to go specific path or end up being extremely over-or underleveled. The game doesn´t give any hints on the difficulty of each challenge either making anyone wanting to go in optimal order to rely on external information. The game being really easy doesn´t help either. While no one expects Pokemon to be difficult, Scarlet takes it one step further. If the player does anything extra between challenges like catching pokemon, battling trainers or exploring the region they inevitably end up being easily overleveled making the fighting really boring, again disincentivizing exploration if wanting any form of challenge.

All in all, Pokemon Scarlet feel like a linear Pokemon game with tech demo open world plastered to it in the late stages of development. It misses every point which makes an open world interesting and honestly would make much better game were it truly made to be linear without the illusion of freedom the half baked open world offers. 

For such an empty world it would at least be expected for it to work properly, but the performance is honestly atrocious and having stable 30fps is a rare occurrence. That could make sense if the game would have a lot going for it, but graphical fidelity and the art style being mediocre at best and draw distance being really bad don´t give any excuses for why it cannot perform at least decently.

Only saving grace for the game is the final area and the story contained there but you have to suffer through the rest of the game making it not really worth the wait. Otherwise the gameplay is exactly as expected from previous games and while not horrible itself, it does little to innovate the stale formula. Over all Pokemon Scarlet is a husk of an open world with no reason of exploration having implemented all of the problems of previous instalments and criticisms of open world formula without offering any solutions to them. As a long time Pokemon fan I am extremely disappointed especially after the previous game; Pokemon Legends Arceus that showed a lot of potential.
I honestly don't know why everyone hates this game. I have more than 150 hours in that game and I have found zero glitches, and all the rivals are really good.

An open world main entry Pokemon game was always the dream when I was younger, so I do love Pokemon Scarlet. It is buggy though and in handheld the game doesn’t run that smoothly! Open world means that you can tackle the gym leaders in any order, though that’s not recommended as they don’t scale based on your Pokémon’s level so there is still an order that needs to be followed. A promising start to the open world style of Pokemon which will surely be refined when the Switch 2 is released.
While the game is laggy and a bit glitchy, it is a decent game, and if it weren’t as laggy, it would probably be a 10, Paldea is a great region with many cool trainers, I do recommend.
It Is A Good Quality Game But Graphic Issues Make Game Worse Although Main Story Is Good
Best pokemon game in ages, everything about this other than the performance issues and the ongoing issue from the last gen of not having a national dex is fantastic.

The gameplay was great, the open world was fun, the characters and the pokemon look great, this game had the best story since black and white,has decent difficulty. I really loved the climax with area 0 which looked great, the pokemon and npcs look amazing and got updated textures. But the games visuals are awful ,there are a ton of bugs, there is no level scaling.  This game is a 6/10 for me.
Stories were kind of meh, gameplay gets pretty repetitive, and the game itself was all kinds of buggy.

Pretty fun but lacks a lot polish. The game is really easy and you can get over levels easily. You can meet pokemon inside of walls sometimes and the camera can be really annoying. Atleast the gameplay is fun

I can only recommend this game to people who are able to tolerate constant frame drops, technical issues, and can make their own fun.

The best moments of scarlet and violet come towards the end. The games have awful performance and issues that make them unenjoyable until the story picks up enough momentum to be interesting. I really liked the storyline in this series. I know, I know, there are some strong technical issues, but I didn't experience them with the handheld switch. I honestly didn't mind the direction they went with this one and really do think the storyline was borderline cinematic.
The new pokemon are all so adorable and the the new features are great welcome to the series

I kinda went into this game knowing about things like it's performance issues, and to be honest, that's my only major downside with this (aside from the lackluster story, but that's an issue with most modern Pokemon Games, so I stopped caring about the story as much and started expecting lackluster stories). This game is genuinely the most fun I've ever had with a Pokemon game, and it's all because of the open world aspect of it. It feels so free, it feels like a real adventure, and the fact that there are rarely any hard roadblocks keeping you out of specific areas (as in unnatural ones, like a random herd of Psyduck blocking the way to a city) makes it feel that much more free. This also kind of helps with the issue of difficulty, but not really. Difficulty almost kept this from getting an 8, but I found that the lack of difficulty was kind of balanced out by how fun it was, so it barely made it. I didn't find a lot of unique strategies used by trainers, because the closest thing to a strategy someone would have is something to counter 1 super-effective type, which is more than some games have, but still... would've liked to have seen more use of held items. And I can't ignore the performance issues. The game can go below 20 fps sometimes, but mostly, it handles 30 to 40FPS well... that is, unless you go in a more crowded area. The crowded areas are so laggy, and I think it's a byproduct of the graphics, which are gorgeous, but gorgeous in the way a poisonous plant can be; yeah, it's pretty to look at... but once it does stuff, it ain't gonna be pretty, that's for sure. Sure, you can just play it on a stronger platform, but I don't know if Nintendo's next console will be backward compatible with the Switch, and if it is, I don't know how much more bearable it can make the lag, but still. I would've given this a 9... but the lackluster story already barred it from a 10, and the performance issues were major enough to knock it down to an 8. I also don't wanna count DLC into this review, but if I did, then this would be a 9. The DLC is so good, but we're talking about the main game. It's the most fun Pokemon game so far, but it's also the worst performing (as in how it runs), and probably the only game where I've ever wanted the graphics to be worse so that I don't have to panic about the game crashing whenever I enter an area with 8 or more breathing organisms. Performance issues aside, this game's fun. I would recommend if you can either handle lag, or if you know of a way to run the game on something stronger.

The game itself is really good! I love the open world aspect and the story and characters, for a Pokemon game, are incredible. However, it's hard to overlook the graphical issues and the lack of level scaling makes it really hard to enjoy the open world. This is a step in the right direction for the Pokemon franchise, but if they really want to tap into the open world thing they have a lot of things to improve on.

I'm a long time Pokemon fan. This Game is verry fun and I have more than 100 hours in this game. AlthoughI I had so much fun, the graffics are terrible and often I just got stuck in the air for 6 seconds. And I'm not even going to start talking about the frame rate.

( I played this game a bit after the actual release so my opinion is on the later versions). Pokémon scarlet/violet is generally a good game. It is definitely a great step into the right direction for future Pokémon games. Its open world is very expansive but has some flaws. When I was playing the game, I didn’t notice any major problems aside from some slight frame drops like in the large lake area in the north west . Even after completing the game and finishing the Pokédex, I didn’t have any visual or physical bugs aside from two small glitches, which didn’t affect my gameplay. While yes, the open world has flaws, I think it’s still amazing.  The story is nothing to scoff at as well, the characters are interesting and refreshing. The story keeps my attention and makes me actually want to see what happens next. I understand why it was released when it was, but I feel that Nintendo should just take their time to make games at full potential. I hope to see great things from the Nintendo company.

I rated this game an 8/10 because although the game is very fun and entertaining, there are still bugs in places. Though even after many bug fixes, it does still lag a bit under certain conditions but not to the point where you cannot work around it. Would definitely recommend.

The open world was very fun and welcoming addition to the pokemon franchise! I hope they use it a lot more! I saw reviews saying it was "un playable" I bought it a week after release and despite the lag in the water it was very playable. Barely glitches and the glitches I did have were ones that didn't affect gameplay! The story was good, great characters, and overall a very fun game! I would recommend this to a lot of people!

I didn't play this on release, so a lot of the potential performance issues experienced were very minimal and more so limited to specific areas of the game. Other than that, I found myself enjoying this title far more than previous ones in many ways. The quality of life improvements from previous entries is significant for those who play more than the initial game. There is a very achievable and manageable endgame for both Competitive Pokemon, and Raids. (PVP and PVE.) The story was shockingly deep and well done, keep in mind this is a presentation for someone more teen-aged. It covers more complex topics that an audience that age could tackle with on a daily basis, while delivering the reliable "feel-good" moral message that Nintendo always does.

The new entries Pokemon wise are about average, there is roughly as many "cool" new ones as there are "uninspired" ones. But, to some extent that is expected as we're now beyond 1,000. 

A number of mechanical improvements, and some level of open world exploration is enjoyable and fun to navigate around. We are closely approaching a way to enjoy a true open world main entry for Pokemon. 

The DLC has been solid so far, about what you should expect for some extra content. 

Overall, it's a safe step forward. While we can agree that we want to see GameFreak be a bit more daring in the future, I cannot say that any parts of this game were particularly disappointing in any way. There are measurable improvements in many aspects of the game, from co-op, to gym progression, to story delivery, to end game PVP and PVE accessibility, and many more.

The one thing I would criticise, and it's more of a stylistic choice than actual mechanical issue is the new "Terastalize" mechanic. I just think it visually looks awful in comparison to Gigantamax or Mega Evolutions. Mechanically it's function is enjoyable and fresh, but visually it is just very unenjoyable.

This game is not even half as bad as people say it is. Everything about this game is amazing except for the graphics and frame rate issues, and I've only ran into one major bug even though I've been playing the game since day 1. Storywise and gameplaywise, it's a masterpiece. The whole game is a mystery. And the end of the game is the most entertaining ending in a pokemon game. Pokemon designs are cool and clearly inspired, not necessarily "just designed to be plushes". The designs have a lot going for them. This game is way underrated. The only bad thing that you'll run into is frame rate issues. I have to admit, that was a huge problem. However, if you can look past these performance issues, you'll be able to enjoy the game and it's clever story for what it's worth. The characters are very relatable and have their own unique personalities too, which will probably make you more intrigued and interested to see what happens in the story. This game has the best writing of any pokemon game, and all of the characters and stories feel real.
I'm not sure what else to say, but I hope that you can look past it's performance issues and realize how engaging, interesting, and compelling the characters, story, and writing are when you try it for yourself. It's definitely worth the money, especially with all of its post-game content as well.
I love it, but honestly, to much pressing (A) haha. Very much action and downloading DLC are bigger than expected.

From the corny story and game of Sword or Shield, this game takes a nicer route. Besides some FPS issues and small nuisance, this game is so much fun and doesn't deserve the review bombing it has gotten. It's actually awesome!

super-mario-bros-wonder_with_metadata.js.review: The only thing that's somewhat lame, making this game not a 9 or a 10, is the 'wonder' system. The first time you play the game and see the wonder effect, it's like, "WOW! This is so cool and wacky!" Then you replay the game again, and it's like, "Eh, I've seen this already; who cares?" Every time you replay the game, it loses its spectacle factor (also, having one save file associated with a Nintendo account instead of multiple save files is pretty dumb, and it SLIGHTLY pisses me off). But aside from all of that, this game is probably one of the best Mario games of all time. Absolutely get this game now!
If it was just a bit longer... I feel like it could be in the top 3 best 2d mario platformers of all time

this is a new take on the try and true NEW super Mario bros formula in a fun way it feels like Nintendo said lets take super Mario 3d world and make a new 2d game based off of that instead of the old Mario games it is a fresh breath of what is a dead genre of 2d platformers and even more specifically a fresh breath into 2d mario
A really great and fun game which makes the super mario bros games fun again, definitely try it out!

I just want to start with that when I first saw the announcement trailer for this game, I had no interest in buying it. After a while, I started to gain more interest in the game and decided to buy it. Wow.This games name is really accurate. It really gives a sense of wonder to all the levels (especially the music levels). I had a feeling of joy the whole time and was only sad when it ended.

The only thing I dislike is that characters do not bump on each other in multiplayer, which in previous games created some of the funniest times i've had with my friends in a videogame. It also makes multiplayer all too easy. Also it would have been nice to be able to ride Yoshi out of single player. Besides that, the developers' boundless creativity in making this game is simply overwhelming. Definitely the best Mario game since the legendary Mario World.
Amazing. You can see that the whole Nintendo creative team was involved in this game. Very, very good.

This game innovates on the New Super Mario formula. It adds new powers and the ability to "wonder" around freely in the world map to select your level. The levels on their own are quite interesting, but the wonder mechanics are what adds a new spin to the series. The wonder fruits allow the developers to let loss and explore new mechanics that will not traditionally fit on a normal Mario experience (like a POV change, or game genre change). The wonder fruits really kept my single player experience enjoyable and worth my time. I will continue to play the game on multiplayer, since is fun and chaotic with my family. I will see if in the future I muster the strength to get the master level courses, but the control set up hurts my thumbs, so I would like a way to customize them. Anyway, overall a great experience.

Super Mario Bros. Wonder is a spectacular evolution of the 2D formula that finally breaks free from the "New" series' predictability. The Wonder Effects inject a sense of joyous unpredictability into every level, constantly subverting expectations with surreal transformations and creative set pieces. With its vibrant art style, expressive animations, and the addition of the Badge system for customizable gameplay, it feels like the most inventive Mario adventure in years. It is a nearly flawless masterpiece of platforming design that belongs in every Switch library.

Well, I think my biggest mistake with this game was having high expectations for a genre that’s already pretty saturated and has titles like Celeste and Rayman Legends, which are both excellent games and cost a lot less. And I think one common mistake Nintendo has been making is releasing games without looking at what competitors are doing, believing that the Mario name alone will be strong enough to sell like crazy — which, unsurprisingly, is exactly what happened with this game. It sold 4 million copies in two weeks, which is pretty insane.

Metroid Dread is another example of a rather weak metroidvania if you compare it to indie games like Hollow Knight or Ender Lilies, which offer a much better experience at a much lower price than Metroid does ($60). Both of these genres are oversaturated in today’s gaming market, which makes it hard to innovate. And I also think Nintendo doesn’t really bother innovating much in these two styles anymore — their focus on innovation seems to be with their 3D games.

Comparing it to the last 2D Mario game, New Super Mario Bros. 2 on the 3DS, Mario Wonder feels like a breath of fresh air. But for me, it still doesn’t surpass what the first New Super Mario Bros. (the one on the DS) meant at the time. Of course, back then the platformer genre wasn’t as overcrowded as it is today. I also think the fact that Wonder’s price doesn’t really match what it delivers ($40 would’ve been more fair) plays a big role too.

And it’s worth mentioning that even though some of the music stages are one of this Mario’s supposed innovations, Rayman Legends does it way better and in a much more memorable way — especially with how it uses well-known songs given a unique twist, like ‘Eye of the Tiger.’ Also, most of the ‘innovations’ that happen with the Wonder Flower have already been done either in other indie games or even by Nintendo itself. For example, the stages with giant shadows — that idea already existed in some levels of Donkey Kong Tropical Freeze. And honestly, in terms of difficulty, Tropical Freeze is way ahead of Mario Wonder.

That pretty much sums up my biggest criticism of Mario Wonder: even though it’s a fun game, it doesn’t innovate in any meaningful way. The only fresh moments come from the Wonder Flower segments. Its short length also makes it hard to recommend at full price ($60) when there are platformers like the two I mentioned before that are longer, cheaper, and more inventive. Still, I’d rate it an 7 out of 10. I think the media reviews were extremely overblown — I honestly feel a lot of those high scores came more from people’s nostalgia than the game’s actual quality. It even got nominated for Game of the Year in 2023, and honestly, titles like Hogwarts Legacy or even Nintendo’s own Pikmin 4 deserved that spot way more.

So, to sum it up, it’s one of the best 2D Mario games, but not an excellent platformer overall — mainly because 2D Mario feels far behind the competition. I’d actually recommend Tropical Freeze if you want a 2D Nintendo platformer, since it goes on sale more often and offers a more fitting challenge for people used to this genre.

But I think Nintendo’s biggest marketing success was leaning heavily on nostalgia and saying in interviews that this would be a sequel to Super Mario World on the SNES (which many consider the best Super Nintendo game ever), while finally trying to create a new formula for Mario since New Super Mario Bros. on the Nintendo DS back in 2006

This is an incredible Mario game.  All of the things you love about classic Mario games are found in this game.  The innovations, and changes such as wonder seeds are zany and imaginative.  The level designs are all so unique, it felt like you were experiencing something brand new every single level, let alone every world.  The characters were charming, and the new Mario character mechanics were fun and useful.  After playing every single main line (side scroller) Mario game, I can confidently say that this is the best one so far without a doubt.  Highly recommend if you like Mario games!

I'm so done with 2D Super Mario games. They were novel before, but even with so many new features, this game just feels so boring and mundane compared to the 3D ones. It's just too many random events and sequences thrown together. Oh yay, another random event. Oh yay, another random sequence. Not really fun anymore.
This has a complete other style in comparison to the other mario bros games but it does NOT dissapoint
This is a very interesting and dinamic game to play for all the public, it's not like the classics game of this saga. This game is different than the others and this is good.

I really enjoyed this game honestly, there was a lot of charm within this 2D Mario and Ilove to see 2D Mario make a comeback, my only complainy is that theres really only 2 Bosses Bowser & Bowser Jr, other then that Solid new entry!

I knew I am bored with 2D Mario but Wonder promised so much. I was mildly entertained for 20 levels, but then I got annoyed by the forced good vibes and the ultimately boring wonder effects. 

It has creativity written all over it, but it‘s just consumption. It feels like dopamine milking until you are dry.

I liked some of the design elements: the sideways, not sideways 3D model of Mario for example.

Definitely a HUGE breath of fresh air from the carbon copies of each other that were the later NSMB games. Vibrant graphics, better job at modernising the 2D Mario formula for it's generation, not to mention the Wonder Seeds being a very neat mechanic. I'd love to see where Nintendo goes with the 2D Mario series from this point, and this game is proof that Nintendo can still make them the way they did back in the 80s and 90s.

Also to me the art direction is honestly not my thing, and the new power ups don’t really do anything for me. I think this game is fine I just fail to see the major appraisal this game receives.
Love this game! The animations looks great and not as boring as the NSMB series. Love that Daisy is a playable character too. The only problem was the boss battles. The were laughuably easy.

Amazing game, I enjoyed new mechanics after years and years after the same super mario bros mechanics and copy pasted games.. it actually gave life to the series! I love the flower wonders, the talking flowers are funny af, the boss fights are fun though the last one is goated, levels are pretty good, badges are PEAK!! Nothing bad to say about this maybe unless they exagerrated with the last level's difficulty, yet i was able to beat it and 100% so I guess it's not a problem for me

ABSOLUTELY PERFECT IN EVERYSINGLE WAY SHAPE OR FORM. I’ve played this game and I can tell you that it is 100% worth it. The coop is great and each level brought me so much fun and joy and I just highly recommend it since it was a…
WONDER

Okay, hear me out here. I don’t think the game is that good. I suppose there is the main mode, but there’s really nothing more to do here. I feel like they overhyped the game, when I eventually got my hands on it, I was just overall dissapointed. It’s not in any means a bad game,I just feel like everyone was saying it’s so great, that it just felt gray. I was expecting a masterpiece, but got something okay at best. It’s not the best game the worlds seen, just face it.

The animation is absolutely phenomenal, the level design is so creative, its like they put a million different ideas they had since the forgettable wii u game into a blender and it came out as an amazing smoothie. 

The way it slowly ramps up in difficulty, world after world is seamless with no difficulty spikes. 

This game also has marvellous spectacle, piranhas will sing, there is rock concerts, you get to play as a goomba, what more could you want?

I suppose my only criticisms are that the map could have been better presented visually and easier to navigate and also i feel like the soundtrack isn't as strong as some other entries in the series.

A truly wonderful game.

Such a breath of fresh air after we’ve been getting just NSMB for the past decade. So much love and care was put into this game with the fantastic visuals, great level design, the great new power ups, literally everything. The only thing is that the bosses are pretty disappointing since you just fight Bowser Jr a bunch of times. The final boss is really good though even if it is easy. I also wish that the game just had multiple save fields instead of having to make new users or deleting your existing save if you want to replay the game. But other than that, this is a fantastic game and I would recommend it.
This is one of the best 2D platformers ever. The feature of the Wonder Flower is really neat and the levels are awesome. I also like that you can play as Luigi, Peach, and even more characters.

I’ve just 100% completed Super Mario Bros. Wonder and it’s easily one of the most refreshing 2D Mario experiences in years. The creativity, level design, and sheer charm kept me hooked from start to finish. The difficulty ramps up beautifully, and while most levels felt fair, I nearly lost my mind (and almost broke my Pro Controller) on Badge Marathon: The Final-Final Test. That challenge alone made the completion so much more rewarding. Score: 10/10

Super Mario Bros. Wonder is a fantastic platforming adventure that, while not the best in the series, is a vital and refreshing step forward.

The game's standout feature is the the "Wonder Flowers." These moments are a masterclass in game design, completely subverting expectations and transforming levels in bizarre, hilarious, and visually spectacular ways. This constant sense of surprise injects a much-needed shot of innovation into the 2D Mario formula. 

The new power-ups, like the incredible Elephant Mario, are also great additions that feel perfectly at home in the Mario **** core platforming is a solid, reliable foundation. It's the classic Mario experience you know and love, with tight controls and satisfying level design that makes every jump feel just right. 

This familiarity is a strength, not a weakness, and it makes the game instantly accessible to both veterans and newcomers. The inclusion of characters like Yoshi and Nabbit, who take no damage, is a thoughtful addition that makes the game highly accessible for children and new players, ensuring that everyone can experience the magic.

However, the game's overall score reflects the fact that it doesn't quite reach the heights of the series' greatest hits. While the Wonder Flowers are brilliant, the traditional platforming sections between them can feel a bit safe and by-the-book. The game’s relatively low difficulty and repetitive boss battles also prevent it from being a truly challenging experience for seasoned **** the end, Super Mario Bros. 

Wonder is a joyous and essential game. It's a testament to the fact that there's still so much room for new ideas within the 2D Mario framework. While it may not dethrone the best games in the franchise, it's an important and incredibly fun entry that successfully revitalizes the series for a new generation.

The only thing that's somewhat lame, making this game not a 9 or a 10, is the 'wonder' system. The first time you play the game and see the wonder effect, it's like, "WOW! This is so cool and wacky!" Then you replay the game again, and it's like, "Eh, I've seen this already; who cares?" Every time you replay the game, it loses its spectacle factor (also, having one save file associated with a Nintendo account instead of multiple save files is pretty dumb, and it SLIGHTLY pisses me off). But aside from all of that, this game is probably one of the best Mario games of all time. Absolutely get this game now!
If it was just a bit longer... I feel like it could be in the top 3 best 2d mario platformers of all time

this is a new take on the try and true NEW super Mario bros formula in a fun way it feels like Nintendo said lets take super Mario 3d world and make a new 2d game based off of that instead of the old Mario games it is a fresh breath of what is a dead genre of 2d platformers and even more specifically a fresh breath into 2d mario
A really great and fun game which makes the super mario bros games fun again, definitely try it out!

I just want to start with that when I first saw the announcement trailer for this game, I had no interest in buying it. After a while, I started to gain more interest in the game and decided to buy it. Wow.This games name is really accurate. It really gives a sense of wonder to all the levels (especially the music levels). I had a feeling of joy the whole time and was only sad when it ended.

The only thing I dislike is that characters do not bump on each other in multiplayer, which in previous games created some of the funniest times i've had with my friends in a videogame. It also makes multiplayer all too easy. Also it would have been nice to be able to ride Yoshi out of single player. Besides that, the developers' boundless creativity in making this game is simply overwhelming. Definitely the best Mario game since the legendary Mario World.
Amazing. You can see that the whole Nintendo creative team was involved in this game. Very, very good.

This game innovates on the New Super Mario formula. It adds new powers and the ability to "wonder" around freely in the world map to select your level. The levels on their own are quite interesting, but the wonder mechanics are what adds a new spin to the series. The wonder fruits allow the developers to let loss and explore new mechanics that will not traditionally fit on a normal Mario experience (like a POV change, or game genre change). The wonder fruits really kept my single player experience enjoyable and worth my time. I will continue to play the game on multiplayer, since is fun and chaotic with my family. I will see if in the future I muster the strength to get the master level courses, but the control set up hurts my thumbs, so I would like a way to customize them. Anyway, overall a great experience.

Super Mario Bros. Wonder is a spectacular evolution of the 2D formula that finally breaks free from the "New" series' predictability. The Wonder Effects inject a sense of joyous unpredictability into every level, constantly subverting expectations with surreal transformations and creative set pieces. With its vibrant art style, expressive animations, and the addition of the Badge system for customizable gameplay, it feels like the most inventive Mario adventure in years. It is a nearly flawless masterpiece of platforming design that belongs in every Switch library.

Well, I think my biggest mistake with this game was having high expectations for a genre that’s already pretty saturated and has titles like Celeste and Rayman Legends, which are both excellent games and cost a lot less. And I think one common mistake Nintendo has been making is releasing games without looking at what competitors are doing, believing that the Mario name alone will be strong enough to sell like crazy — which, unsurprisingly, is exactly what happened with this game. It sold 4 million copies in two weeks, which is pretty insane.

Metroid Dread is another example of a rather weak metroidvania if you compare it to indie games like Hollow Knight or Ender Lilies, which offer a much better experience at a much lower price than Metroid does ($60). Both of these genres are oversaturated in today’s gaming market, which makes it hard to innovate. And I also think Nintendo doesn’t really bother innovating much in these two styles anymore — their focus on innovation seems to be with their 3D games.

Comparing it to the last 2D Mario game, New Super Mario Bros. 2 on the 3DS, Mario Wonder feels like a breath of fresh air. But for me, it still doesn’t surpass what the first New Super Mario Bros. (the one on the DS) meant at the time. Of course, back then the platformer genre wasn’t as overcrowded as it is today. I also think the fact that Wonder’s price doesn’t really match what it delivers ($40 would’ve been more fair) plays a big role too.

And it’s worth mentioning that even though some of the music stages are one of this Mario’s supposed innovations, Rayman Legends does it way better and in a much more memorable way — especially with how it uses well-known songs given a unique twist, like ‘Eye of the Tiger.’ Also, most of the ‘innovations’ that happen with the Wonder Flower have already been done either in other indie games or even by Nintendo itself. For example, the stages with giant shadows — that idea already existed in some levels of Donkey Kong Tropical Freeze. And honestly, in terms of difficulty, Tropical Freeze is way ahead of Mario Wonder.

That pretty much sums up my biggest criticism of Mario Wonder: even though it’s a fun game, it doesn’t innovate in any meaningful way. The only fresh moments come from the Wonder Flower segments. Its short length also makes it hard to recommend at full price ($60) when there are platformers like the two I mentioned before that are longer, cheaper, and more inventive. Still, I’d rate it an 7 out of 10. I think the media reviews were extremely overblown — I honestly feel a lot of those high scores came more from people’s nostalgia than the game’s actual quality. It even got nominated for Game of the Year in 2023, and honestly, titles like Hogwarts Legacy or even Nintendo’s own Pikmin 4 deserved that spot way more.

So, to sum it up, it’s one of the best 2D Mario games, but not an excellent platformer overall — mainly because 2D Mario feels far behind the competition. I’d actually recommend Tropical Freeze if you want a 2D Nintendo platformer, since it goes on sale more often and offers a more fitting challenge for people used to this genre.

But I think Nintendo’s biggest marketing success was leaning heavily on nostalgia and saying in interviews that this would be a sequel to Super Mario World on the SNES (which many consider the best Super Nintendo game ever), while finally trying to create a new formula for Mario since New Super Mario Bros. on the Nintendo DS back in 2006

This is an incredible Mario game.  All of the things you love about classic Mario games are found in this game.  The innovations, and changes such as wonder seeds are zany and imaginative.  The level designs are all so unique, it felt like you were experiencing something brand new every single level, let alone every world.  The characters were charming, and the new Mario character mechanics were fun and useful.  After playing every single main line (side scroller) Mario game, I can confidently say that this is the best one so far without a doubt.  Highly recommend if you like Mario games!

I'm so done with 2D Super Mario games. They were novel before, but even with so many new features, this game just feels so boring and mundane compared to the 3D ones. It's just too many random events and sequences thrown together. Oh yay, another random event. Oh yay, another random sequence. Not really fun anymore.
This has a complete other style in comparison to the other mario bros games but it does NOT dissapoint

I knew I am bored with 2D Mario but Wonder promised so much. I was mildly entertained for 20 levels, but then I got annoyed by the forced good vibes and the ultimately boring wonder effects. 

It has creativity written all over it, but it‘s just consumption. It feels like dopamine milking until you are dry.

I liked some of the design elements: the sideways, not sideways 3D model of Mario for example.

Definitely a HUGE breath of fresh air from the carbon copies of each other that were the later NSMB games. Vibrant graphics, better job at modernising the 2D Mario formula for it's generation, not to mention the Wonder Seeds being a very neat mechanic. I'd love to see where Nintendo goes with the 2D Mario series from this point, and this game is proof that Nintendo can still make them the way they did back in the 80s and 90s.

Look, after years of copy-pasted New Super Mario Bros platformers, it's hard to complain too much about Super Mario Bros Wonder. The entire central conceit is built around solving the central criticism: 2D Mario games have become derivative. And indeed, playing through the game is a breezy joy. The art style is top-notch, the new enemy designs are fantastic, and some of the Wonder Seed moments are truly inspired. 

That said, I can't help but feel like the game builds a solid foundation for Mario's future, but doesn't quite feel like the future itself. It introduces a lot of fun elements but doesn't push it to quite the next level as we might be used to from Mario's 3D games. I think a perfect example are the new power-ups, namely the elephant and the drill. Both are so much fun to use, but feel surprisingly limited in what they allow you to do. Even compared with the cape from Super Mario World, let alone the moveset from the 3D games, it just feel like the player is as empowered as they should be to play around with these new tools. I will also say that I found the Wonder Seeds to be a bit hit-or-miss. Some of them are so clever and fun that others can't help but feel like missed opportunities in comparison. 

For my money, the best new element are the badges that meaningfully change up the way you control these characters. This is exactly the kind of freedom of choice I wish there was more of from the other features of the game. But even here, I get the sense that the development team could have pushed the limits a bit more, perhaps peppering levels with secret paths based on which badges you used. This certainly would have helped with replay-ability, because as it is the game is too brief and most levels aren't so inspired as to compel you to go through them again unless you are already a compulsive completionist.

There are other gripes here and there -- other than the final level the game is not the least bit challenging -- but it's important to restate that any fan of Mario and platformers in general should still play this game. It is a step in the right direction and a lovely experience to binge through in a weekend or two. The art style and animation in particular deserves all the praise in the world. It's just one of those experiences where I spent a lot of my playtime dreaming about what the sequel will do.

Unfortunately, I must say that I am disappointed given the price the game demands.First of all, the level design is quite appealing. The graphics and creative environments immediately draw you into Mario's world. However, the game falls short in terms of content. The challenge is overall very low, which significantly detracts from the gaming experience.Another major point of criticism is the boss battles. These are not only unimaginative but also disappointingly easy to defeat. With a playtime of only 8-10 hours, I find the price-to-value ratio unjustifiable.

I do not recommend this game. The multiplayer is awful, the music is very forgettable, and the level design is utter trash. Levels feel barren and empty without the wonder seeds, and the platforming is brain dead. The wonder seeds become very predictable after the first few levels, and the ideas they introduce are never expanded upon. Ideas are abandoned as soon as they are introduced,  and it feels as if tiktok was a video game. Also, the controls are a general downgrade from the previous entry in the series, as Mario's signature momentum based controls are abandoned in favor of megaman style controls, where the character can change directions at a whim and instantly be going at max speed in that direction.

This game is a steaming pile of garbage. The greedy developers are charging $60 for a 2D platformer. Maybe this game would be fine if it were $30-40 but $60 is extremely expensive. When I pay $60 I expect top-notch AAA quality. I'm sick of these developers taking shortcuts while still having the audacity to slap these insane price tags. Although I do get nostalgic for 2D Mario games, so maybe I'll have fun, right? See that's the freaking problem, the game becomes less Mario the further you progress. They even replaced the 1985 Mario power-up sound when you hit the box. It feels like I'm playing Rayman Legends or some indie 2D trash. The game started out fine, but once that first musical sequence came into play, it was all downhill from there. And don't even get me started with that second musical that had awful fps in the background. Does Nintendo think we're all toddlers? Mario has existed since the freaking 80s, how old do you think some of us are? Yeah I get, Mario is for kids, but that doesn't mean you have to add this cringeworthy Cocomelon Baby Shark nonsense. As if Mario as an elephant wasn't cringey enough, he says "wowie zowie" when he turns into one. Wowie Zowie? All this cringe made me want to snap my Switch in half. ● For goodness sake, why are these developers so obsessed with making Bowser the main antagonist? Enough already. You think by now that stupid turtle/dragon would've given up by now after failing for the gazillionth time. How am I suppose to take a perennial loser seriously? It's a new kingdom, so now is the perfect time to introduce a new villain. The developers heavily advertised the Wonder Flower effects, which is nothing more than a gimmick that gets old fast. How about they spend time actually improving the gameplay instead of coming up with gimmicks. Mario (any character) takes too long to drop the flag. The levels are boring and repetitive. The boss fights are trash. ● The online multiplayer is lazily designed. The so-called live shadows aren't always in real-time. A lot of them are just time trial ghosts. Speaking of time trials, the friend races are a joke. I am not a big fan of non-contact racing, because it's pretty much just a time-trial. There is one thing I did enjoy about non-contact racing in a game like GTA V for example, and that's seeing other players get intimidated by my driving and choking as a result. There is no way I can intimidate random people when there's no matchmaking. Why can't I get a Mario vs Luigi PvP online mode instead? Just imagine how fun this game would be if it had like 10 players or so battling against each collecting coins. That would be biblical, but the developers are too cheap and lazy for that. I could probably look past the shortcomings if that were the case, but until then it's garbage. The talking flowers are annoying and so are the jump sounds. The local multiplayer is the only way to experience the same level together, but it's just co-op for the stupid campaign. Developers these days are too greedy and lazy to give me original content for co-op or PvP modes. I don't recommend this game at all. ● P.S. Out of all the reviews you deleted Metacritic, you actually had a point with this one. I said there was no indication for fail pits and called it "guesswork". I guess I was wrong about that but the indication is garbage either way. I deleted that part of my review, so what's your excuse now? Allow me make myself more clear. I am well aware of the black shadowy thing on the fail pits. The problem is the black shadow bar is present in inaccessible pits, making it look like a cosmetic thing rather than an indicator for death. How was I suppose to know? It doesn't help that it was too dark in the "secret room" and secret pipe area for me to notice it, especially when I lowered the brightness to save battery. On top of that, these developers added the most worthless death pits in Mario history, because there are many pits you can't possibly fail unless you do so on purpose. Nintendo, you need to add better indication, or better yet just do it the old-fashioned way for goodness sake. From now on, I'll just review your games from a TV standpoint.

Very mediocre Mario game. Levels are way too easy(and optional), too many powerups to help you, you can buy lives(!). Then there's the annoying flowers, uninspired music and an open world that adds nothing. You are better off playing the older nes/snes games.

But, controls feel weird, and this game is notfunto play with more than one person on a single switch, one random player gets the fullscreen and the rest just have to pray that they can find their characters.

Maybe it's because I grew up in the 80s and 90s and remember how perfectly fun and difficult those early side scrollers were and how accomplished a felt at beating a level, let alone the game.  

This game is the opposite of that.  Literally nothing is difficult to the point that it feels like you are just going through the motions.  Mario was never about story so having essentially no story and levels that are, while graphically interesting, ultimately boring af, means that I can't give this more than a 4/10.  Playing with my husband after taking a gummy maybe I could bump it up to a 6/10 .temporarily.  But that's because we are fun, not the game.

Even with the magical seeds I feel it's a very simple and plain Super Mario game with low difficulty and few secrets. Maybe I'm getting old (I'm 40) but I still prefer Super Mario World, Mario 64, Yoshi's Island, Super Mario 3D World, Odyssey and other Mario titles.

Creative and fun at times but some unfair design decisions and imprecise controls are in stark contrast with Nintendo’s typical “hand-holdy” approach render this a very uneven experience, even frustrating sometimes.

Perhaps I have been too critical of Nintendo's games this year, or perhaps a flurry of great games coming out in 2023 has made me reassess how good Nintendo is. While I do enjoy some of Super Mario Bros. Wonder, I find a lot of its content just misses what makes Mario platforming enjoyable. The wonder flowers are an interesting idea, but in practice just take away from the core platforming. The wonder seeds are so easy to acquire that they make collectibles in games feel boring. I spent most of my playthrough in multiplayer, and some optional side levels are broken in multiplayer, such as in one where you must search an open level for tokens, but one is hidden down a pit. My partner jumped in the pit and died. It wasn't until 10 minutes later that we searched up the solution and found out that we were right, but the game didn't like it because the wrong player jumped down the pit. Perhaps its just the multiplayer, or perhaps the entire game isn't that good, but Super Mario Bros. Wonder for me was a thoroughly middling and joyless experience.
In almost every way, it’s better than its “New” predecessors, but for the entirety of my 10 hour play-though, one thought persisted in my mind—I’d rather be playing Tropical Freeze.
Good ideas, nice worlds but an awful control converts what could have been a really cool experience into a very frustrating one. Not recommended.
Should be a really good game, but for me, it's above all a multiplayer game, and they really kill the pleasure to play due to the crown system. If you don't have the cown, this game is a real pain...

Not as good as the old Mario’s. Not even close. Super Mario revolutionized video games when it reached its apex, SM3 and SM world, because it showed us videogame is not only quick and pointless button clicking, but also exploration, discovery and secrets. Sure, there were hard parts, but with an average level of skill and intelligence/creativity one can uncover all the secrets and complete everything. But SM Wonder has very few really good secrets; the main courses are short and easy, and the big coins and secret exits found almost effortlessly. The badge challenges and special world are extremly hard when it comes to skill, but pointless. For those who really like pure brainless skill, there is Super Mario Maker, which I never liked. I ended all the main courses, found all the seeds and coins in less than one week, and for the first time I dont feel like doing the 100% in a mainline Mario Game, because the badges and specials are so boring. I miss New SM; despite old graphics and ideas, felt much more like SM 3/World.
Solid, fresher, fun, good sounds and…. It’s a Mario bros so it’s familial!

Nothing fun about losing over and over and over, because an enemy does something unexpected or there's no way through a section. Nintendo should be ashamed of this mess.

Super Mario Bros Wonder manages to disappoint on multiple fronts, as its glaring flaw lies in its startling lack of difficulty throughout the entire gameplay experience. From start to finish, the game offers little more than a tedious and uninspiring journey, failing to engage players who crave the thrill of overcoming challenging obstacles. While the game attempts to showcase a variety of creative ideas, the absence of a substantial difficulty curve renders these concepts feeble at best. It's as if the developers decided to cater exclusively to a toddler demographic, leaving more seasoned gamers  Each level feels like a mere stroll through an overly forgiving landscape, eliminating any sense of achievement or satisfaction that typically accompanies conquering a well-designed platformer. Even as the game boasts a diverse cast, it squanders the potential for a more engaging experience by refusing to introduce any meaningful challenges. The promise of variety is overshadowed by the monotonous ease with which players can progress through each level, culminating in an unimpressive and anticlimactic short endgame. In retrospect, Super Mario Bros Wonder falls short of its potential, leaving players with a taste of unmet expectations. The tepid difficulty levels dilute any positive aspects the game might have had, rendering it an uninspired addition to the Mario franchise. A significant overhaul of the difficulty mechanics is imperative if the game is to be anything more than a forgettable, lackluster entry in the iconic series.

When Mario 2d with the same format, less content and mobility than the previous ones but with 2 new items,Apparently the critics praise Mario for being Mario and not for the product he offers, Mario takes out any garbage and they give him a 90, this is how 40 year old critics treat him..........

This isn't in any way an evolution or expansion of 2D Mario platformers, it is just the same kid-centric, banal, "New" series in a slightly different coat of paint.  There is no unifying central game mechanic, no power ups that are used in interesting ways, no new or interesting music.  It is boring and unchallenging.  If it weren't for the per level "acid trip" segments, the levels would absolutely play themselves.

I just don't understand the hype with this game.  Mario Maker 2 is more of an evolution in terms of what players can do and play and the vast majority of that game's content is user-generated.  I'll give Wonder a few points for style and artistic flair, but that's it.  Nintendo nowadays is afraid of making anything that doesn't cater to the lowest common denominator.  Back in the day they made platformers that were fun, fair, and challenging, but now...this is just junk.

Kirby's Dreamland deluxe is way better than this walk in the park.  Not Mario worthy at all.
Too kiddy, too easy, too scripted.

I do not recommend this game. The multiplayer is awful, the music is very forgettable, and the level design is utter trash. Levels feel barren and empty without the wonder seeds, and the platforming is brain dead. The wonder seeds become very predictable after the first few levels, and the ideas they introduce are never expanded upon. Ideas are abandoned as soon as they are introduced,  and it feels as if tiktok was a video game. Also, the controls are a general downgrade from the previous entry in the series, as Mario's signature momentum based controls are abandoned in favor of megaman style controls, where the character can change directions at a whim and instantly be going at max speed in that direction.
Terrible. Felt like the game was on drugs with intentionally weird gameplay. Waste of money.

Incredibly disappointing sequel. I really enjoyed the first game and maybe the first fifth of this game. It quickly rehashes a lot of ideas the first game had and never really feels like a true sequel. It also forces you to do a lot more BS to get to the ending as just finishing all the unique level stars isn't enough, you have to do almost all the prankster stars as well.

Some of the level design is  made for a character that controls much better than Mario. A decent amount of trial and error that is just frustrating. This comes from unclear goals or just poor level design. The game also is a step down in theming. Rosalina's cool space station ship from the first game is gone, and now you have a tiny Mario head that takes about 2 minutes to explore and there isn't much here. Even the level select is lazier as it is just a tradition Mario path through levels instead of the cool galaxy view they had in the first game.

For the boss fights, like the first game, a lot of repeats. Some stage fights are decent and unique, but the big Bowser fights all repeat. Even the last fight is a repeat fight, just with one extra bonus easy phase to it. But thinking back, Bowser fights have always been repeated in these 3D Mario games and never that good really.

Overall, while there are some decent levels and the game looks great for a Wii game, there is just an abundance of issues here I cannot overlook. Even the decent levels are like B, B-'s. I was rating levels in my head and I think maybe 1 level out of the 71 stars I got was an A tier level. Lots of D's in there and that doesn't make a great game. 6.3/10

A fantastic follow-up to one of my favorite games of all time! I slightly prefer the first one, probably because it was so revolutionary, and felt so new.
But the inclusion of Yoshi as well as many new innovative stages, makes this game as much of a must play as the first one!

It's an improvement on its predecessor in every way, and I appreciate the ideas behind it, it's clearly one of the best platformers ever made. However, being forced to use the Wii's motion controls didn't convince me. Sometimes it felt imprecise and got tiring, plus I've noticed I'm less and less inclined to play platformers, I don't enjoy them as much as I used to.

Super Mario Galaxy 2 is one of the finest 3D platformers ever made—packed with unforgettable moments, clever design, and that unmistakable Mario magic. It's a must-play for fans of the genre and one of the best Mario games of all time.

I found this better than the first game. The gameplay was extremely well done, and it's definitely a fun game to play with family members!

RamRam: I rate it a 10 because it is my favorite Mario game of all time. I like everything from the difficulty, music, levels, and nostalgia I have from the game.
How can video games get more beloved than this? They might as well try, but this will forever be in the hall of fane until abolished.
Masterpiece to this day, even on the switch 2. There is no other game like the mario galaxy games.
In comparison to the first installment, the atmosphere is not that punching, but the gameplay is better and with more interesting ideas. Still a bit linear though.
Expanding on the original while also being great as a stand alone title, totally recommend
The best mario game i play in my life, i start to play this game in the wii when i have 5 years old and its amazing

Incredibly disappointing sequel. I really enjoyed the first game and maybe the first fifth of this game. It quickly rehashes a lot of ideas the first game had and never really feels like a true sequel. It also forces you to do a lot more BS to get to the ending as just finishing all the unique level stars isn't enough, you have to do almost all the prankster stars as well.

Some of the level design is  made for a character that controls much better than Mario. A decent amount of trial and error that is just frustrating. This comes from unclear goals or just poor level design. The game also is a step down in theming. Rosalina's cool space station ship from the first game is gone, and now you have a tiny Mario head that takes about 2 minutes to explore and there isn't much here. Even the level select is lazier as it is just a tradition Mario path through levels instead of the cool galaxy view they had in the first game.

For the boss fights, like the first game, a lot of repeats. Some stage fights are decent and unique, but the big Bowser fights all repeat. Even the last fight is a repeat fight, just with one extra bonus easy phase to it. But thinking back, Bowser fights have always been repeated in these 3D Mario games and never that good really.

Overall, while there are some decent levels and the game looks great for a Wii game, there is just an abundance of issues here I cannot overlook. Even the decent levels are like B, B-'s. I was rating levels in my head and I think maybe 1 level out of the 71 stars I got was an A tier level. Lots of D's in there and that doesn't make a great game. 6.3/10

A fantastic follow-up to one of my favorite games of all time! I slightly prefer the first one, probably because it was so revolutionary, and felt so new.
But the inclusion of Yoshi as well as many new innovative stages, makes this game as much of a must play as the first one!

It's an improvement on its predecessor in every way, and I appreciate the ideas behind it, it's clearly one of the best platformers ever made. However, being forced to use the Wii's motion controls didn't convince me. Sometimes it felt imprecise and got tiring, plus I've noticed I'm less and less inclined to play platformers, I don't enjoy them as much as I used to.

Super Mario Galaxy 2 is one of the finest 3D platformers ever made—packed with unforgettable moments, clever design, and that unmistakable Mario magic. It's a must-play for fans of the genre and one of the best Mario games of all time.

A really good game I love it as well been just I'd still prefer the first one more though but I still think this game is still a must play the first one is like Mankind's greatest creation and the two is like the sequel to Mankind's Greatest creation

There's a lot that it improves from Galaxy 1, but overall this is basically just 'more super mario galaxy'. There are new ideas, new mechanics, new levels. Sometimes it exceeds the heights of its predecessor, but its not nearly as innovative as Galaxy 1 overall.
Not bad, but far, far from perfect. Around halfway or 2/3 through, the game gets a lot grindier than the previous half.

I love that this game feels so similar to the first one. Honestly in terms of 120 stars, I love them both equally. I don’t however, like the green stars. They feel tedious and boring. Maybe a few green stars are ok but 120 of them. Jeez. I wish they’d make the bonus 120 stars a bit more diverse. The red star power up is coded in the game, why couldn’t a chunk of the 120 bonus stars be red power stars. Having flying missions would be so much fun. Maybe introduce a blue star that gets obtained by challenges involving pull stars I don’t know. I just find the green stars very boring. They really should have mixed it up a bit.
